title: Species designation for the Evangelions
wikitext:
[]: Species designation for the Evangelions.             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 10, 2012 8:29 pm If the Evangelions are technically "giant human beings", would they theoretically have a species designation? In NGE, the Angels (and deducibly the Evangelions) have a 99.89% genetic similarity to Homo sapiens /Lilin. Adam in particular is referred to as the first human being in NGE's context, and this is where the Evangelions also inherit this designation. Did NERV/SEELE ever categorize the Evas as a species when they were first developed? I'll put in my own fanwankery for this, with... Nevermind. this is probably starting to far-fetched even for an Eva forum. Just "Evangelion Kastsuragii" maybe? Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 10, 2012 8:55 pm The concept of a species is a bit arbitrarily defined in biology science circles to begin with. However, as a general rule, they're categorized around notions of self-replication. The Evas are clones of the bioengineered Seeds, whose fundamental purpose is not self -replication (which would be simply creating more Seeds), but generating life (especially "human" life) and the planetary conditions under which it can thrive. Odds are that the Seeds are bioengineered variants of the First Ancestral Race themselves (creating god in one's own image, etc.). Hence, they (and the Evas) would best be assigned to some "variation" under the FAR's species name... whatever that ought to be. They're not actually hominids, let alone Homo , so we'd go for a new genus name wholesale.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:00 pm I've always thought that 'human' referred to the presence of a soul or what not rather than actually being part of the genus Homo . Especially, while most Evangelions are from Adam, however Babylon (Eva 01) is from Lilith, and Adam and Lilith are not equivalent. And your Latin is clearly wrong, that would be Homo adae. ( Source )

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:10 pm Lavinius wrote: I've always thought that 'human' referred to the presence of a soul or what not rather than actually being part of the genus Homo .

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:17 pm In the sense that the Lilin became the litmus test for what defines a human may only be due their physical form (hominids). Had humanity developed along a different path (such as the Angels), then it all goes back to the question of what the FAR were like. The Angels, although they don't look like the Lilin, still do to some degree have similar souls (as shown NGE) to Lilin, except the Lilin just happened to grow into the shape of their progenitor, Adam. If a species designation were hypothetically given, I'd now probably suggest "Evangelion" as their genus. I'm fanwanking too much with this topic!

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:32 pm Well, if you really want to get into the taxonomy of an evangelion, I can do that for you. There are some problems with certain stages of the taxonomy pertaining to physical traits of the evas. I don't see any evidence that they are mammals, for example. Also, there is some doubt as to whether or not they are the same sort of life form, due to differences in blood color among the different evas. That's not something that is lightly set aside in taxonomy (in spite of what Star Trek would like you to think). I'd be more inclined to think that an evangelion, being cloned from Lilith, would be more of an example of convergent evolution (and that term is at best used loosely with cloned creatures) than any sort of real similarity between humans and evangelions. As for a species designation, this would be difficult, as the name of each stage of taxonomy is a descriptor of a physical trait. For example, "homo" (human genus) stands for "homonid", means "stands upright" (roughly). This poses problems if any stage above genus is different than it is in the homo genus. You can't have a non-chordata animal belonging to the homo genus. However, in light of the genetic similarity we are given, I am willing to set all this aside and just say that it is loosely human. Now, assuming that evangelions follow human taxonomy down to that level (I have my doubts, but it is implied heavily in the series) that the species designation would very likely be "homo evangelion" simply due to the upright stance of the things.

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:40 pm Tribblepoo wrote: Even if all of the above were true for evangelions, there has to be some explanation as to why the evas exhibit wildly different physical characteristics (namely number and arrangement of eyes).

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:42 pm Replicator wrote: Exactly. That's why there's a problem with the Adam/Lilith clone theory. EVA Unit-02 has 4 eyes and bleeds purple unlike the other Units. Was this ever included within the limits of the Seeds? Especially when you compare the Evas (which are more of clones of extraterrestrials than having any direct relation to Terran life), it's difficult to draw lines where the definitions fall neatly. "Lilin" may be the Angel designation (as with Adam's Children) of Homo sapiens , but technically speaking, wouldn't all of Lilith's descendants still fall under the term in some form?

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:45 pm I remember seeing "crystalline DNA" somewhere before that's probably NGE's in-universe explanation for the properties of the Angels and Evas. To some extent, this structure could mimic what LCL-based life's elements, for example, the Angels have red blood, which in the Lilin is due to the presence of iron (But LCL doesn't include minerals?). http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10293/Rebuild-Angel-biology-notes-speculations/

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:54 pm Replicator wrote: I remember seeing "crystalline DNA" somewhere before that's probably NGE's in-universe explanation for the properties of the Angels and Evas. To some extent, this structure could mimic what LCL-based life's elements, for example, the Angels have red blood, which in the Lilin is due to the presence of iron (But LCL doesn't include minerals?). http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10293/Rebuild-Angel-biology-notes-speculations/

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 10, 2012 11:09 pm Replicator wrote: In the sense that the Lilin became the litmus test for what defines a human may only be due their physical form (hominids). Had humanity developed along a different path (such as the Angels), then it all goes back to the question of what the FAR were like.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Mon Dec 10, 2012 11:13 pm This is why the field of taxonomy was, in my opinion, invented by someone with a rather severe case of OCD.
